cases.name: fastapi_python_ceiling
cases.query: After FastAPI added Python 3.14 support, what became the newest explicitly supported Python line?
cases.citation_first_answer: FastAPI 0.119.0 added support for Python 3.14, while Python 3.13 still remained supported in the same later release-note stream. FastAPI higher-end Python support after the next upgrade.
cases.raw_local_model_answer: FastAPI 0.119.0 became the newest explicitly supported Python line.
cases.local_model_answer: Python 3.14 became the newest explicitly supported Python line.

cases.name: fastapi_pydantic_migration
cases.query: What changed for direct Pydantic v1 support in later FastAPI releases?
cases.citation_first_answer: FastAPI later dropped support for Pydantic v1, first keeping only short temporary support for Pydantic v2's pydantic.v1 and then dropping pydantic.v1 support outright.

conflicts.message: Another active source with overlapping evidence was retrieved. This answer may need manual comparison across citations.

cases.raw_local_model_answer: FastAPI later dropped support for Pydantic v1, first keeping only short temporary support for Pydantic v2's pydantic.v1 and then dropping pydantic.v1 support outright.
cases.local_model_answer: FastAPI later dropped support for Pydantic v1, first keeping only short temporary support for Pydantic v2's pydantic.v1 and then dropping pydantic.v1 support outright.
